page: Amazon Web Services r8g.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local NVMe storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25% and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 108.05 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also show strong performance, with gzip compression at 30.6 GB/sec. However, raw CPU compute and specialized instruction performance are weaker, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU score in the bottom 25% and poor performance in encryption tests. LLM inference speeds are also weak, generating 6.5 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

The r8g.xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads due to its high resource density and efficient ARM-based architecture. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and memory-intensive applications that do not require local storage or heavy floating-point computations.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8g.xlarge is an ARM-based, memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal strong memory performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though raw CPU compute and encryption capabilities are relatively weak. With 8.0 GB of memory per core, the r8g.xlarge is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and general memory-bound applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.xlarge is an ARM-based memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and strong memory bandwidth performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.xlarge is an ARM-based memory-optimized instance with 4 dedicated cores, 32 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM-powered, memory-optimized instance offering high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, delivering 8.0 GB of memory per core
Strong memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-bound workloads
